name: using-beads-bv
description: Use when coordinating dependency-aware task planning with beads (bd) CLI and bv graph sidecar - covers ready work selection, priority management, and robot flags for deterministic outputs
capabilities: Find issues ready to work using `bd ready` command
View issue details with dependencies via `bd show <id>`
View graph metrics including PageRank and critical path with `bv --robot-insights`
Create and manage dependent issues with `bd create` and `bd dep add`
Claim and close work items with `bd update` and `bd close`
author: AgentWorkforce
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown guidance for using beads/bv CLI tools. No executable code, no network calls, no file system access, no command execution. All 61 static findings are false positives from misinterpreting markdown command examples as executable backtick syntax.
valueStatement: Manage complex multi-agent workflows with dependency tracking. Beads provides a lightweight issue database where bd CLI handles task management and bv delivers graph metrics for execution planning with deterministic robot flags.
limitations: Requires beads and bv CLI tools to be installed locally
Interactive TUI will block sessions without using `--robot-*` flags
Does not replace TodoWrite for simple single-session tasks
Depends on proper beads database setup for dependency tracking

useCases.title: Coordinate agent task dependencies
useCases.description: Plan and track work across multiple AI agents with clear dependency chains and parallel execution paths

useCases.title: Manage sprint execution metrics
useCases.description: Use bv graph insights to identify critical path items and optimize team workflow sequencing

useCases.title: Track priority work allocation
useCases.description: Apply P0-P4 numeric priorities and visualize what work items unblock other deliverables

promptTemplates.title: Find ready work
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all issues ready to work using beads

promptTemplates.title: Get execution plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an execution plan for the current sprint using bv

promptTemplates.title: Create dependent tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue for feature X and another for its tests, then make the tests depend on the feature

promptTemplates.title: View graph insights
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the project graph metrics including critical path and any cycles

bestPractices: Always use `--robot-*` flags with bv to avoid blocking TUI sessions
Use P0-P4 numeric priorities instead of high/medium/low labels
Include beads issue IDs (e.g., bd-123) in all commit messages for traceability
Run `bd sync` before ending any session to commit changes
antiPatterns: Using bv without robot flags and getting stuck in interactive TUI
Managing tasks in markdown files instead of beads database
Missing issue IDs in commit messages breaks traceability
Using high/medium/low priority labels instead of P0-P4 format

faq.question: What are the P0-P4 priority levels?
faq.answer: P0 blocks everything (critical), P1 needs immediate attention, P2 is standard work, P3 is nice to have, P4 is future backlog.

faq.question: How do I avoid blocking in bv?
Can I use this for single-agent tasks?
faq.answer: No. Use TodoWrite for simple single-session tasks. Beads is designed for multi-agent coordination with dependencies.

faq.question: How do I create dependent issues?
faq.answer: Create both issues with `bd create`, then link them with `bd dep add <depends-on-id> <issue-id>`.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: This is a documentation-only skill. It describes how to use beads/bv CLI tools which access your local beads database.

faq.question: How is this different from TodoWrite?
faq.answer: TodoWrite is for personal task tracking. Beads is for shared, dependency-aware task management across multiple agents and sessions.
